title: Study shows red wine biomarkers in urine can predict inflammation levels
summary: Researchers found that specific metabolites of resveratrol in urine accurately reflect red wine consumption. These biomarkers were linked to reduced inflammation in older adults at cardiovascular risk, unlike traditional food surveys.

title: Spinach Benefits, Nutrition Facts and Recipes to Try
summary: Can eating spinach regularly improve your health? Learn the health benefits of spinach, its nutrition facts and ways to enjoy this leafy green.

title: AI can help doctors give intravenous nutrition to preemies
summary: An algorithm that learned from tens of thousands of nutrition prescriptions for premature babies could reduce medical errors and better identify what nutrients the smallest patients need.

title: Epigenetic scores offer new insights into cardiometabolic disease prevention
summary: A new study explores how epigenetic markers-chemical modifications that influence gene activity-can help predict the risk of developing cardiometabolic diseases and guide personalized prevention strategies.

title: Study reveals distinct infection strategies of Zika and dengue viruses
summary: Zika virus and dengue virus are very close relatives. Both are mosquito-borne flaviviruses, and both specialize in infecting a host's dendritic cells.

title: Why Mānuka isn't the only healing honey from New Zealand anymore
summary: New research from Auckland University of Technology reveals that eight native New Zealand honeys, beyond mānuka, possess unique antioxidant, antibacterial, and bioactive properties. Thyme and beech honeydew stood out for their vitamin C and polyphenol content.

title: Oklahoma researcher uses technology to combat alcohol use disorder in primary care
summary: According to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, 1 in 7 adults in the United States will experience a substance use disorder during their lifetime. University of Oklahoma College of Medicine faculty member Brandi Fink, Ph.D., is working with primary care clinics and health care systems to identify people with an alcohol use disorder and intervene early before the problem worsens.

title: Who was Katie Donnell? Heart attack kills Florida ‘workout queen' at 28, and her mom blames energy drinks
summary: Katie Donnell, 28, was committed to a healthy lifestyle but often drank three energy drinks a day, and “was drinking a lot of coffee,” according to her mother.

title: Stanford study reveals glucose's surprising role in cell differentiation
summary: The sugar glucose, which is the main source of energy in almost every living cell, has been revealed in a Stanford Medicine study to also be a master regulator of tissue differentiation - the process by which stem cells give rise to specialized cells that make up all the body's tissues.

title: Researchers unlock key process for regenerating intestinal cells after injury
summary: Researchers from the University of Colorado Cancer Center have solved a cellular mystery that may lead to better therapies for colorectal and other types of cancer.
